meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Solve Slow Development
content.seo_description: Stop wasting weeks on boilerplate. Learn how to leverage SaaS starter kits, Antigravity, and Claude Code to ship production-ready apps at record speed.
content.answer_block: Stop building authentication, billing, and database schemas from scratch. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you reclaim 40+ hours per project. Combine this with Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation, allowing you to focus purely on high-value business logic and client-specific features.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking with the rhythmic mockery of a deadline closing in. You know the stack—Next.js, Supabase, Stripe—but the sheer weight of setting up the boilerplate, configuring webhooks, and wiring up authentication feels like building a car just to drive to the grocery store. Every hour spent on infrastructure is an hour stolen from your bottom line.

But the best in the business don't build from zero. They leverage battle-tested SaaS starter kits to bypass the 'plumbing' phase entirely. By integrating AI-driven workflows, you transform your development process from manual craftsmanship into a high-velocity assembly line. It’s time to stop reinventing the wheel and start shipping products that actually move the needle for your clients.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Define your SaaS foundation using Antigravity to ensure your directory structure follows industry-standard patterns for scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a secure, scalable SaaS architecture using Next.js 14, TypeScript, and Tailwind. Include modular folders for services, hooks, and components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate initial project structure based on Antigravity best practices'

steps.title: Auth & Database Integration
steps.description: Automate the connection between your application and Supabase/Clerk to ensure secure user management without writing custom auth logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure authentication schema and middleware for Supabase integration, ensuring RLS policies are strictly enforced.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement user authentication flow using Supabase and Clerk'

steps.title: Billing Logic Implementation
steps.description: Stripe integration is notoriously error-prone; use AI to verify webhook handlers and subscription plan structures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust Stripe webhook handler for subscription events including checkout.session.completed and invoice.payment_succeeded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add Stripe subscription logic and webhook endpoints'

steps.title: Component Library Injection
steps.description: Rapidly build your UI by injecting pre-tested, accessible components that match your client's design system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable landing page component set using Radix UI and Tailwind CSS, focused on high conversion rates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate landing page components following the design system'

steps.title: Automated Testing & QA
steps.description: Ensure code integrity by running automated tests on your core business logic before deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Playwright test suites for the critical path: Auth, Subscription Checkout, and Profile Update.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate and execute Playwright tests for the critical user journey'

pro_tips: Always store your boilerplate configuration as a git-ignored template so you can re-apply it to new projects without 'pollution' from old client code.
Use Claude Code to perform 'diff-based' refactoring; never ask it to rewrite the whole file, only the specific module that needs optimization.
Prioritize 'feature-flagging' early in your starter kit so you can deploy incomplete features to production without breaking the UX.
Configure your CI/CD pipeline to automatically run your Antigravity linting rules on every push to catch architectural drift before it becomes technical debt.
Maintain a private 'component garden' repository that Claude Code can index, allowing you to pull in your own bespoke UI patterns consistently.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is configured, perform a dry-run deployment to Vercel or your preferred cloud provider. Monitor the initial build time and refine your prompts to reduce overhead further.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to ship in days what used to take weeks. Stop the manual grind and start building your empire. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
